name: Mahatma Gandhi
fact1: Trained as a lawyer abroad, he returned home to lead a struggle for self-rule
fact2: He pioneered mass nonviolent resistance, from a march to make salt to boycotts of foreign goods
fact3: His campaigns helped India win independence, and he was assassinated soon after

name: Winston Churchill
fact1: A writer and politician, he later won a Nobel Prize for Literature
fact2: Known for stirring speeches, he rallied a nation enduring heavy bombing
fact3: He led Britain as prime minister through most of the Second World War

name: Abraham Lincoln
fact1: Largely self-educated, he rose from a frontier log cabin to high office
fact2: He led his country through a devastating civil war
fact3: He issued the Emancipation Proclamation and was assassinated in a theater

name: Martin Luther King Jr.
fact1: A Baptist minister, he became the voice of a movement for equality
fact2: He preached nonviolent protest and led marches and boycotts
fact3: His 'I Have a Dream' speech and assassination made him a civil rights icon

name: Isaac Newton
fact1: A solitary, prickly genius, he made breakthroughs during a plague lockdown
fact2: He invented a branch of mathematics and built a reflecting telescope
fact3: His laws of motion and universal gravitation founded classical physics

name: Galileo Galilei
fact1: He improved a new optical instrument to study the night sky
fact2: His observations of moons and phases supported a Sun-centered universe
fact3: He was tried by the Inquisition and forced to recant his astronomy

name: William Shakespeare
fact1: A glovemaker's son, he became an actor and part-owner of a theater company
fact2: He wrote dozens of plays staged at a famous riverside playhouse
fact3: His works, from Hamlet to Macbeth, reshaped the English language

name: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
fact1: A child prodigy, he toured European courts performing before age ten
fact2: He composed prolifically across symphonies, operas, and concertos before dying young
fact3: His works, like a famous Requiem and The Magic Flute, define the Classical era

name: Ludwig van Beethoven
fact1: A composer of fiery temperament, he kept creating despite a cruel affliction
fact2: He continued writing masterpieces even after going profoundly deaf
fact3: His Ninth Symphony, ending in an 'Ode to Joy', is among the most famous music ever

name: Charles Darwin
fact1: A young naturalist, he spent years on a voyage around the world
fact2: Studying finches and tortoises on remote islands shaped his thinking
fact3: He proposed evolution by natural selection in On the Origin of Species

name: Julius Caesar
fact1: A patrician general, he was also a gifted writer of his own war memoirs
fact2: He conquered Gaul and crossed a river with his army to seize power
fact3: Declared dictator, he was stabbed to death by senators on the Ides of March

name: Alexander the Great
fact1: Tutored by a famous philosopher, he became king of a Greek realm as a youth
fact2: He never lost a battle while marching his army thousands of miles east
fact3: He built an empire from Greece to India before dying at 32

name: Aristotle
fact1: He studied under a renowned thinker before founding his own school
fact2: He wrote on everything from logic and biology to ethics and poetry
fact3: He tutored Alexander the Great and shaped Western thought for centuries

name: Plato
fact1: Born to an aristocratic family, he turned from politics to philosophy
fact2: He founded an influential Academy and wrote in the form of dialogues
fact3: A student of Socrates, his Republic imagined an ideal just society

name: Socrates
fact1: He wrote nothing himself; we know him through his students
fact2: He questioned citizens relentlessly to expose gaps in their reasoning
fact3: Condemned by Athens, he died by drinking hemlock

name: Cyrus the Great
fact1: He founded one of the ancient world's largest empires
fact2: Unusually, he was known for tolerating conquered peoples' religions
fact3: He founded the Achaemenid Persian Empire and freed exiles in Babylon

name: Ashoka
fact1: A powerful emperor, he first expanded his realm through brutal conquest
fact2: Horrified by a bloody war, he converted to a peaceful faith
fact3: He spread Buddhism across India and carved edicts on pillars and rocks

name: Qin Shi Huang
fact1: He ended an age of warring states by uniting them under one rule
fact2: He standardized writing, currency, and even cart-axle widths
fact3: China's first emperor was buried with a vast terracotta army

name: Gautama Buddha
fact1: Born a prince, he left a life of luxury to seek the meaning of suffering
fact2: After years of wandering, he found enlightenment meditating under a tree
fact3: His teachings on ending suffering became the foundation of Buddhism

name: Muhammad
fact1: A merchant known for honesty, he often retreated to meditate in a cave
fact2: He united the tribes of the Arabian Peninsula under a new faith
fact3: Muslims regard him as the final prophet who received the Quran

name: Jesus
fact1: A traveling teacher, he gathered a small group of devoted followers
fact2: He taught in parables and was said to perform miracles
fact3: His crucifixion and the belief in his resurrection founded Christianity

name: Martin Luther
fact1: A monk and professor, he grew troubled by practices of the church
fact2: He reportedly nailed a list of grievances to a church door
fact3: His 95 Theses sparked the Protestant Reformation

name: Joan of Arc
fact1: A teenage peasant girl, she said she heard divine voices
fact2: She led soldiers to victory during a long war between two kingdoms
fact3: Captured and tried, she was burned at the stake and later made a saint

name: Elizabeth I
fact1: Daughter of a much-married king, she survived imprisonment to take the throne
fact2: She never married, presiding over a golden age of theater and exploration
fact3: Her navy defeated the Spanish Armada during her long reign

name: Christopher Columbus
fact1: He convinced a royal court to fund a risky westward voyage
fact2: Seeking a route to Asia, he reached lands unknown to Europeans
fact3: His 1492 voyages opened the Americas to European colonization

name: Marco Polo
fact1: As a young man, he set out on an overland journey to the East
fact2: He spent years in the court of a great Mongol emperor
fact3: His book about travels to China fascinated medieval Europe

name: Ferdinand Magellan
fact1: He led an expedition seeking a westward sea route to the spice islands
fact2: He found a strait at the tip of South America that now bears his name
fact3: His expedition first circled the globe, though he died along the way

name: James Cook
fact1: A skilled navigator, he rose through the navy from humble roots
fact2: He led three voyages mapping the Pacific in unprecedented detail
fact3: He charted New Zealand and Australia's east coast before dying in Hawaii

name: Amelia Earhart
fact1: She set numerous records in a then-new mode of travel
fact2: She was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic
fact3: She vanished over the Pacific attempting to fly around the world

name: Neil Armstrong
fact1: A test pilot and engineer, he flew experimental aircraft
fact2: He commanded a historic space mission in 1969
fact3: He was the first human to walk on the Moon

name: Nikola Tesla
fact1: An eccentric inventor, he claimed to picture machines fully formed in his mind
fact2: He championed a form of electrical current that powers the modern grid
fact3: He pioneered alternating current and feuded with Thomas Edison

name: Thomas Edison
fact1: With little formal schooling, he amassed over a thousand patents
fact2: He built one of the first industrial research laboratories
fact3: He developed a practical light bulb and the phonograph

name: Alexander Graham Bell
fact1: He worked teaching the deaf, including his own mother and wife
fact2: His experiments with sound led to a world-changing device
fact3: He is credited with inventing the telephone

name: Louis Pasteur
fact1: A chemist, he showed that tiny organisms spoil food and cause disease
fact2: He developed a gentle heating process to make liquids safe
fact3: He created vaccines for rabies and anthrax; pasteurization bears his name

name: Florence Nightingale
fact1: Born to a wealthy family, she defied expectations to pursue caregiving
fact2: She organized care for wounded soldiers during a war, walking the wards at night
fact3: Known as the Lady with the Lamp, she founded modern nursing

name: Mother Teresa
fact1: She left her homeland young to become a nun and teacher
fact2: She devoted her life to the poorest and dying in a large Indian city
fact3: She founded the Missionaries of Charity and won the Nobel Peace Prize

name: Queen Victoria
fact1: She became queen at 18 and reigned for over six decades
fact2: Her era saw vast industrial growth and a sprawling empire
fact3: She gave her name to the Victorian age and ruled at the empire's height

name: Catherine the Great
fact1: A foreign-born princess, she seized power from her own husband
fact2: An admirer of Enlightenment ideas, she expanded her empire and patronized the arts
fact3: She was Russia's longest-ruling empress

name: Tutankhamun
fact1: He became a ruler as a young boy and died in his teens
fact2: A minor king in his time, he was largely forgotten for millennia
fact3: The 1922 discovery of his intact tomb made him world-famous

name: Hatshepsut
fact1: She ruled as one of the very few female pharaohs
fact2: She often had herself depicted with the symbols of a male king
fact3: She launched grand building projects and trade expeditions in ancient Egypt

name: Mansa Musa
fact1: He ruled a vast and immensely wealthy West African kingdom
fact2: On a famous pilgrimage, he gave away so much gold he disrupted economies
fact3: The emperor of Mali is often called the richest person in history

name: Shaka Zulu
fact1: He transformed a small clan into a powerful military force
fact2: He revolutionized warfare with new weapons and tactics
fact3: He founded and led the mighty Zulu kingdom

name: Haile Selassie
fact1: He was the last emperor of an ancient African kingdom
fact2: He appealed to the world for help when his country was invaded
fact3: He is revered as a messianic figure in the Rastafari movement

name: Kwame Nkrumah
fact1: He studied abroad before returning to lead an independence movement
fact2: He became the first leader of his newly independent nation
fact3: Ghana's founding leader, he was a champion of pan-Africanism

name: Simón Bolívar
fact1: Born to wealth, he was inspired by revolutionary ideas in Europe
fact2: He led armies that freed several nations from colonial rule
fact3: Called the Liberator, a whole country is named after him

name: Che Guevara
fact1: Trained as a doctor, a motorcycle journey radicalized him
fact2: He became a guerrilla leader in a Caribbean revolution
fact3: His image on a beret became a global symbol of rebellion

name: Frida Kahlo
fact1: A bus accident left her in lifelong pain and shaped her art
fact2: She painted many striking, symbolic self-portraits
fact3: Her bold self-portraits made her a Mexican art icon

name: Pablo Picasso
fact1: A prodigy who could draw before he could speak, he reinvented his style often
fact2: He co-founded a movement that broke objects into geometric planes
fact3: He pioneered Cubism and painted the anti-war mural Guernica

name: Vincent van Gogh
fact1: He sold almost no paintings during his troubled, short life
fact2: He worked in bold colors and thick, swirling brushstrokes
fact3: He painted The Starry Night and famously cut off part of his ear

name: Michelangelo
fact1: He considered himself a sculptor above all else
fact2: He spent years painting a vast ceiling lying on his back
fact3: He sculpted David and painted the Sistine Chapel

name: Johann Sebastian Bach
fact1: From a large musical family, he held church and court posts his whole life
fact2: He composed intricate works for organ and choir, underappreciated in his day
fact3: His Baroque masterpieces include the Brandenburg Concertos

name: Rumi
fact1: A scholar and teacher, a profound friendship transformed him into a poet
fact2: He wrote ecstatic verse about divine love that inspired whirling dervishes
fact3: His mystical Persian poetry makes him a best-selling poet even today

name: William the Conqueror
fact1: A duke from across the Channel, he claimed a contested throne
fact2: He won a decisive battle in the year 1066
fact3: He conquered England and ordered the Domesday survey

name: Charlemagne
fact1: He united much of western Europe under one crown
fact2: He promoted learning during an age often called dark
fact3: He was crowned the first Holy Roman Emperor on Christmas Day, 800

name: Suleiman the Magnificent
fact1: He led a powerful empire to its golden age
fact2: A lawgiver and patron of architecture, he also conquered widely
fact3: He was the longest-reigning Ottoman sultan

name: Akbar
fact1: He expanded an empire across much of the Indian subcontinent
fact2: Though unable to read, he promoted religious tolerance and the arts
fact3: He was the greatest of the Mughal emperors

name: Kublai Khan
fact1: A grandson of a famous conqueror, he ruled a huge realm
fact2: He hosted European travelers at his lavish court
fact3: He founded the Yuan dynasty and ruled China as emperor

name: Saladin
fact1: A Muslim leader respected even by enemies for his chivalry
fact2: He united Muslim forces during an era of religious wars
fact3: He recaptured Jerusalem from the Crusaders

name: Hannibal
fact1: He swore lifelong hostility to a rising Mediterranean power
fact2: He famously led war elephants across the Alps
fact3: The Carthaginian general nearly defeated Rome itself

name: Spartacus
fact1: Once a soldier, he was enslaved and trained as a gladiator
fact2: He escaped and led a massive uprising of the enslaved
fact3: His revolt against Rome ended in crucifixions along a road

name: Stephen Hawking
fact1: A physicist, he kept working despite a paralyzing disease
fact2: He communicated through a computerized voice
fact3: He explained black holes and wrote A Brief History of Time

name: Ada Lovelace
fact1: Daughter of a famous poet, she was steered toward mathematics
fact2: She worked on plans for an early mechanical computing engine
fact3: She is often called the first computer programmer

name: Alan Turing
fact1: A brilliant mathematician, he imagined a universal computing machine
fact2: He helped crack enemy codes during a world war
fact3: A father of computer science, he was persecuted for being gay

name: Nicolaus Copernicus
fact1: A church official, he studied the heavens in his spare time
fact2: He proposed a radical rearrangement of the cosmos
fact3: He placed the Sun, not Earth, at the center of the universe

name: Johannes Gutenberg
fact1: A goldsmith, he tinkered with metal and ink
fact2: His invention made books cheap and widespread
fact3: He introduced the movable-type printing press to Europe

name: Benjamin Franklin
fact1: A printer by trade, he was also a writer, scientist, and diplomat
fact2: He experimented with electricity using a kite in a storm
fact3: A founding father, he helped draft the US Declaration of Independence

name: George Washington
fact1: A surveyor and planter, he became a military commander
fact2: He led an army to victory in a war for independence
fact3: He became the first president of the United States

name: Thomas Jefferson
fact1: A polymath, he designed his own hilltop mansion
fact2: He was the principal author of a famous founding document
fact3: He wrote the Declaration of Independence and became the third US president

name: Mao Zedong
fact1: A librarian's assistant, he became a revolutionary leader
fact2: He led a long march and a communist revolution
fact3: He founded the People's Republic of China in 1949

name: Vladimir Lenin
fact1: A radical thinker, he spent years in exile
fact2: He led a revolution that toppled an empire
fact3: He founded the Soviet Union

name: Karl Marx
fact1: A journalist and philosopher, he lived much of his life in exile and poverty
fact2: He analyzed class struggle and the workings of capitalism
fact3: He co-wrote The Communist Manifesto and Das Kapital

name: Adam Smith
fact1: A moral philosopher, he lectured in Scotland
fact2: He described how self-interest can guide markets as if by an invisible hand
fact3: His Wealth of Nations founded modern economics

name: Sigmund Freud
fact1: A doctor, he became fascinated with the hidden mind
fact2: He treated patients by exploring their dreams and childhood
fact3: He founded psychoanalysis and theories of the unconscious

name: Rosa Parks
fact1: A seamstress, she was active in a civil rights group
fact2: Her quiet act of defiance sparked a citywide bus boycott
fact3: She refused to give up her bus seat to a white passenger

name: Harriet Tubman
fact1: Born into slavery, she escaped to freedom
fact2: She risked her life returning to guide others north
fact3: She led many to freedom on the Underground Railroad

name: Malcolm X
fact1: He turned his life around in prison through study and faith
fact2: He became a fiery spokesman for Black empowerment
fact3: He advocated Black nationalism before being assassinated

name: Susan B. Anthony
fact1: A reformer, she campaigned against slavery and alcohol
fact2: She was arrested for voting illegally
fact3: She led the fight for women's suffrage in America

name: Emmeline Pankhurst
fact1: She founded a militant women's organization
fact2: She and her followers were jailed and went on hunger strikes
fact3: She led the British suffragette movement

name: Anne Frank
fact1: A young girl, she was given a diary for her birthday
fact2: She hid with her family for two years during a war
fact3: Her diary from hiding during the Holocaust is read worldwide

name: Mark Twain
fact1: A former riverboat pilot, he took his pen name from river slang
fact2: He wrote humorous, sharp-eyed novels of American life
fact3: He wrote Tom Sawyer and Huckleberry Finn

name: Jane Austen
fact1: She wrote in relative obscurity, published anonymously
fact2: Her witty novels explored marriage and social class
fact3: She wrote Pride and Prejudice

name: Charles Dickens
fact1: Forced to work in a factory as a child, he never forgot the poor
fact2: He published serialized novels that gripped readers month to month
fact3: He wrote A Christmas Carol and Oliver Twist

name: Leo Tolstoy
fact1: Born a count, he later renounced much of his wealth
fact2: He wrote sweeping novels of Russian society
fact3: He wrote War and Peace and Anna Karenina

name: Homer
fact1: Little is known about him; he may not have been one person
fact2: He is credited with the foundational epics of Western literature
fact3: He composed the Iliad and the Odyssey

name: Dante Alighieri
fact1: Exiled from his city, he wrote in the common tongue instead of Latin
fact2: He imagined a journey through the afterlife
fact3: He wrote The Divine Comedy

name: Miguel de Cervantes
fact1: A soldier who lost the use of a hand in battle and was once enslaved
fact2: He wrote what many call the first modern novel
fact3: He created Don Quixote

name: Murasaki Shikibu
fact1: A noblewoman, she served at an imperial court
fact2: She wrote about court life and romance
fact3: She wrote The Tale of Genji, often called the first novel

name: Sun Tzu
fact1: A general and strategist in ancient China
fact2: He wrote a short, enduring treatise on conflict
fact3: He authored The Art of War

name: Pythagoras
fact1: He led a secretive community devoted to numbers
fact2: He believed numbers underpinned the whole universe
fact3: A famous theorem about right triangles bears his name

name: Archimedes
fact1: He reportedly shouted 'Eureka!' after a discovery in his bath
fact2: He devised machines, levers, and a way to measure volume
fact3: The Greek mathematician founded much of physics and was killed by a Roman soldier

name: Avicenna
fact1: A child prodigy, he mastered medicine while still a teenager
fact2: He wrote an encyclopedia of medicine used for centuries
fact3: His Canon of Medicine was a standard text across Europe and the Islamic world

name: Ibn Battuta
fact1: He set out on a pilgrimage and then kept traveling for decades
fact2: He journeyed across Africa, Asia, and Europe
fact3: His travels covered far more distance than Marco Polo's

name: Zheng He
fact1: A court official, he commanded enormous fleets
fact2: He led seven grand voyages across the Indian Ocean
fact3: His treasure ships dwarfed European vessels of the age

name: Mary Shelley
fact1: Daughter of pioneering writers, she eloped as a teenager
fact2: A ghost-story contest on a rainy holiday inspired her masterpiece
fact3: At 18 she began writing Frankenstein

name: Henry Ford
fact1: A farm boy fascinated by machines, he founded a car company
fact2: He made automobiles affordable for ordinary families
fact3: He pioneered the moving assembly line

name: John D. Rockefeller
fact1: He started as a bookkeeper and built a vast fortune
fact2: He came to dominate an entire industry through one company
fact3: His Standard Oil made him history's wealthiest oil baron

name: Steve Jobs
fact1: A college dropout, he started a company in a garage
fact2: Famed for design and showmanship, he was ousted and later returned
fact3: He co-founded Apple and launched the iPhone

name: Pelé
fact1: He grew up poor, practicing with a sock stuffed with rags
fact2: He became a national hero in a sport played mostly with the feet
fact3: He won three World Cups for Brazil in football

name: Muhammad Ali
fact1: Brash and poetic, he changed his name after converting to Islam
fact2: He refused military service and was stripped of his title
fact3: He was a three-time world heavyweight boxing champion

name: Jesse Owens
fact1: The grandson of slaves, he was a record-breaking athlete
fact2: He competed at an Olympics a dictator hoped to use for propaganda
fact3: He won four gold medals at the 1936 Berlin Olympics
